public int numberOfStableArrays(int zero, int one, int limit) {

    // Compute f for zero and one, given limit.

    // Precompute f for zero.

    int[][] fZero = computeF(zero, limit);

    int[][] fOne = computeF(one, limit);

    // Now, for all k and l.

    long total = 0;

    for (int k = 1; k <= zero; k++) {

        for (int l = 1; l <= one; l++) {

            if (Math.abs(k - l) > 1) continue;

            int interleave;

            if (k == l) {

                interleave = 2;

            } else if (k == l + 1) {

                interleave = 1;

            } else {

                interleave = 1;

            }

            total += fZero[k] * fOne[l] * interleave;

        }

    }

    return (int) (total % MOD);

}

private int[][] computeF(int n, int limit) {

    int[][] f = new int[n+1][]; // f[i] will be for i.

    for (int i = 0; i <= n; i++) {

        int maxK = i == 0 ? 1 : i; // for i=0, k can be 0.

        f[i] = new int[maxK + 1];

        for (int k = 0; k <= maxK; k++) {

            if (k == 0) {

                if (i == 0) {

                    f[i][k] = 1;

                } else {

                    f[i][k] = 0;

                }

            } else {

                if (i < k) {

                    f[i][k] = 0;

                } else {

                    f[i][k] = 0;

                    for (int j = 1; j <= limit && j <= i - (k-1); j++) {

                        f[i][k] += f[i - j][k - 1];

                        f[i][k] %= MOD;

                    }

                }

            }

        }

    }

    return f;

}

public int numberOfStableArrays(int zero, int one, int limit) {

    // Compute f for zero and one, given limit.

    // Precompute f for zero.

    int[][] fZero = computeF(zero, limit);

    int[][] fOne = computeF(one, limit);

    // Now, for all k and l.

    long total = 0;

    for (int k = 1; k <= zero; k++) {

        for (int l = 1; l <= one; l++) {

            if (Math.abs(k - l) > 1) continue;

            int interleave;

            if (k == l) {

                interleave = 2;

            } else if (k == l + 1) {

                interleave = 1;

            } else {

                interleave = 1;

            }

            total += fZero[k] * fOne[l] * interleave;

        }

    }

    return (int) (total % MOD);

}

private int[][] computeF(int n, int limit) {

    int[][] f = new int[n+1][]; // f[i] will be for i.

    for (int i = 0; i <= n; i++) {

        int maxK = i == 0 ? 1 : i; // for i=0, k can be 0.

        f[i] = new int[maxK + 1];

        for (int k = 0; k <= maxK; k++) {

            if (k == 0) {

                if (i == 0) {

                    f[i][k] = 1;

                } else {

                    f[i][k] = 0;

                }

            } else {

                if (i < k) {

                    f[i][k] = 0;

                } else {

                    f[i][k] = 0;

                    for (int j = 1; j <= limit && j <= i - (k-1); j++) {

                        f[i][k] += f[i - j][k - 1];

                        f[i][k] %= MOD;

                    }

                }

            }

        }

    }

    return f;

}

Alternatively, perhaps a helper function can compute f for a given n and limit.

So, the helper function would be:

private void computeF(int[][] f, int n, int limit) {

    for (int i = 0; i <= n; i++) {

        for (int k = 0; k <= i; k++) {

            if (k == 0) {

                if (i == 0) {

                    f[i][k] = 1;

                } else {

                    f[i][k] = 0;

                }

            } else {

                if (i < k) {

                    f[i][k] = 0;

                } else {

                    f[i][k] = 0;

                    for (int j = 1; j <= limit && j <= i - (k-1); j++) {

                        if (i - j >=0 && (k-1) <= (i -j)) {

                            f[i][k] += f[i - j][k - 1];

                            f[i][k] %= MOD;

                        }

                    }

                }

            }

        }

    }

}
